summary: The board of food and grocery delivery company Eternal has approved a proposal to cap foreign ownership in the firm at 49.5%. This move is aimed at providing 'greater operational flexibility' to its quick commerce unit, Blinkit, by allowing it to hold inventory, rather than operate solely as a marketplace. As of March 31, Eternal's domestic ownership stood at 55%. The IOCC (Indian-Owned-and-Controlled Company) status will enable Blinkit to improve its margins, particularly in fragmented or unbranded categories, as well as in the established fast-moving consumer goods segment, where owning inventory allows for better margins. The proposal is subject to shareholders' approval. This move comes after Eternal (then Zomato Ltd) had raised Rs 8,500 crore through a qualified institutional placement (QIP) in November 2024, primarily from domestic mutual funds, which increased the proportion of Indian shareholding in the company. Blinkit's rival, Zepto, is also working to increase its domestic shareholding, having raised $350 million from Indian high-net-worth individuals, family offices and major financial institutions in November 2024 and is currently in talks to raise an additional $250 million.
key_points: The board of Eternal has approved a proposal to cap foreign ownership in the firm at 49.5%
The key stakeholders are Eternal, the parent company of Blinkit, and Blinkit, the quick commerce unit
Eternal had raised Rs 8,500 crore through a qualified institutional placement (QIP) in November 2024, primarily from domestic mutual funds
The proposal is subject to shareholders' approval and is aimed at providing 'greater operational flexibility' to Blinkit by March 31, 2023

The IOCC (Indian-Owned-and-Controlled Company) status will enable Blinkit to improve its margins, particularly in fragmented or unbranded categories, as well as in the established fast-moving consumer goods segment

Blinkit's rival, Zepto, is also working to increase its domestic shareholding, having raised $350 million from Indian high-net-worth individuals, family offices and major financial institutions in November 2024 and is currently in talks to raise an additional $250 million
The move is in line with India's foreign direct investment (FDI) regulations, which require online marketplaces to operate as pure platforms without holding inventory
The increased domestic ownership is expected to have a positive impact on Blinkit's operations and competitiveness in the quick commerce market
Blinkit will be able to hold inventory and operate more flexibly, which could lead to improved margins and operational efficiency
Eternal and Blinkit may explore further strategic initiatives to capitalize on the IOCC status and strengthen their position in the quick commerce market

consensus: Eternal's board has approved a proposal to cap foreign ownership in the firm at 49.5%
The move is aimed at providing 'greater operational flexibility' to Blinkit, Eternal's quick commerce unit
Eternal's domestic ownership stood at 55% as of March 31, 2023
